mn52:1.2: At one time Venerable Ānanda was dwelling at Vesālī, in Beluva village.
mn52:1.3: Now at that time the householder Dasama from Aṭṭhakanāgara had arrived at Pāṭaliputta on some business.
mn52:10.1: with a mind imbued with gladness [*muditā*] …
mn52:11.1: with a mind imbued with equanimity [*upekkhā*] he dwells pervading one direction, likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth. Thus above, below, across, everywhere, and in every respect, he dwells pervading the entire world with a mind imbued with equanimity [*upekkhā*], abundant, expansive, measureless, free from hostility, free from ill will.
mn52:11.3: ‘This liberation of mind through equanimity [*upekkhā*] [*upekkhācetovimutti*] too is constructed and intended.
mn52:11.4: But whatever is constructed and intended, that is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], subject to cessation [*nirodhadhamman*].’ He understands it thus.
mn52:11.6: … attains the unsurpassed security from bondage.
mn52:12.1: Furthermore, householder, a *bhikkhu*, with the complete transcending of saññās of *rūpa* [*rūpasaññānaṁ*], with the vanishing of saññās of resistance [*paṭighasaññānaṁ*], through non-attention to saññās of diversity [*nānattasaññānaṁ*], thinking, ‘Space is boundless,’ enters and dwells in the dimension of boundless space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*].
mn52:12.3: ‘This attainment of the dimension of boundless space [*ākāsānañcāyatanasamāpatti*] too is constructed and intended.
mn52:12.4: But whatever is constructed and intended, that is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], subject to cessation [*nirodhadhamman*].’ He understands it thus.
mn52:12.6: … attains the unsurpassed security from bondage.
mn52:13.1: Furthermore, householder, a *bhikkhu*, by completely going beyond the dimension of boundless space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*], thinking, ‘Consciousness [*viññāṇa*] is boundless,’ enters and dwells in the dimension of boundless consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*].
mn52:13.3: ‘This attainment of the dimension of boundless consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanasamāpatti*] too is constructed and intended.
mn52:13.4: But whatever is constructed and intended, that is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], subject to cessation [*nirodhadhamman*].’ He understands it thus.
mn52:13.6: … attains the unsurpassed security from bondage.
mn52:14.1: Furthermore, householder, a *bhikkhu*, by completely going beyond the dimension of boundless consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*], thinking, ‘There is nothing,’ enters and dwells in the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*].
mn52:14.3: ‘This attainment of the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanasamāpatti*] too is constructed and intended.
mn52:14.4: But whatever is constructed and intended, that is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], subject to cessation [*nirodhadhamman*].’ He understands it thus.
mn52:14.5: Established there, he attains the destruction of the *āsavas*.
mn52:14.6: If he does not attain the destruction of the *āsavas*, then through that very passion for the *dhamma*, through that delight in the *dhamma*, with the destruction of the five lower fetters he becomes one of spontaneous rebirth, and there he attains final *nibbāna*, not liable to return from that world.
mn52:14.7: This too, householder, is one *dhamma* taught by that *Bhagavā*—who knows and sees, the arahant, the perfectly awakened one—such that, for a *bhikkhu* dwelling diligent, ardent, and resolute, the unliberated mind [*cittaṁ*] is liberated, the unexhausted *āsavas* go to exhaustion, and the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained.”
mn52:15.1: When this was said, the householder Dasama from Aṭṭhakanāgara said this to Venerable Ānanda:
mn52:15.2: “Just as, *bhante* Ānanda, a man seeking a single entrance to a treasure might at once find eleven entrances to treasures;
mn52:15.3: so too, *bhante*, while seeking a single door to the deathless, I have at once gained eleven doors to the deathless for development [*bhāvanāya*].
mn52:15.4: Just as, *bhante*, a man’s house might have eleven doors, and if that house were on fire, he could make himself safe by any one of those doors;
mn52:15.5: so too, *bhante*, by any one of these eleven doors to the deathless I will be able to make myself safe.
mn52:15.6: When even followers of other sects will seek a teacher’s fee for their teacher, why should I not make an offering to Venerable Ānanda?”
mn52:16.1: Then the householder Dasama from Aṭṭhakanāgara assembled the *bhikkhu saṅgha* [*bhikkhusaṅghaṁ*] from Pāṭaliputta and Vesālī and personally satisfied and served them with fine staple and non-staple foods,
mn52:16.2: and he clothed each *bhikkhu* individually with a pair of cloths, and Venerable Ānanda with a triple robe, and he had a monastery worth five hundred built for Venerable Ānanda.
mn52:16.3: The Man from Aṭṭhakanāgara is completed, the second.
mn52:2.1: Then the householder Dasama from Aṭṭhakanāgara went to the Cock’s Park, to a certain *bhikkhu*. Having gone to that *bhikkhu*, he paid homage to him and sat down to one side. Seated to one side, the householder Dasama from Aṭṭhakanāgara said this to that *bhikkhu*:
mn52:2.3: For we desire to see that Venerable Ānanda.”
mn52:2.4: “That Venerable Ānanda is dwelling at Vesālī, householder, in Beluva village.”
mn52:3.1: Then the householder Dasama from Aṭṭhakanāgara, having concluded that business in Pāṭaliputta, went to Vesālī, to Beluva village, to Venerable Ānanda. Having gone to Venerable Ānanda, he paid homage to him and sat down to one side. Seated to one side, the householder Dasama from Aṭṭhakanāgara said this to Venerable Ānanda:
mn52:3.2: “Is there, *bhante* Ānanda, a single *dhamma* taught by that *Bhagavā*—who knows and sees, the arahant, the perfectly awakened one—such that, for a *bhikkhu* dwelling diligent, ardent, and resolute, the unliberated mind [*cittaṁ*] is liberated, the unexhausted *āsavas* go to exhaustion, and the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained?”
mn52:3.3: “There is, householder, a single *dhamma* taught by that *Bhagavā*—who knows and sees, the arahant, the perfectly awakened one—such that, for a *bhikkhu* dwelling diligent, ardent, and resolute, the unliberated mind [*cittaṁ*] is liberated, the unexhausted *āsavas* go to exhaustion, and the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained.”
mn52:3.4: “But what, *bhante* Ānanda, is the single *dhamma* taught by that *Bhagavā*—who knows and sees, the arahant, the perfectly awakened one—such that, for a *bhikkhu* dwelling diligent, ardent, and resolute, the unliberated mind [*cittaṁ*] is liberated, the unexhausted *āsavas* go to exhaustion, and the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained?”
mn52:4.1: “Here, householder, a *bhikkhu*, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful *dhammas*, enters and dwells in the first *jhāna*, which has thought [*vitakka*] and examination [*vicāra*], with *pīti* and *sukha* born of seclusion [*viveka*].
mn52:4.3: ‘This first *jhāna* too is constructed and intended.
mn52:4.4: But whatever is constructed and intended, that is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], subject to cessation [*nirodhadhamman*].’ He understands it thus.
mn52:4.5: Established there, he attains the destruction of the *āsavas*.
mn52:4.6: If he does not attain the destruction of the *āsavas*, then through that very passion for the *dhamma*, through that delight in the *dhamma*, with the destruction of the five lower fetters he becomes one of spontaneous rebirth, and there he attains final *nibbāna*, not liable to return from that world.
mn52:4.7: This too, householder, is one *dhamma* taught by that *Bhagavā*—who knows and sees, the arahant, the perfectly awakened one—such that, for a *bhikkhu* dwelling diligent, ardent, and resolute, the unliberated mind [*cittaṁ*] is liberated, the unexhausted *āsavas* go to exhaustion, and the unsurpassed security from bondage not yet attained is attained.
mn52:5.1: Furthermore, householder, a *bhikkhu*, with the stilling of thought [*vitakka*] and examination [*vicāra*], with inner clarity … enters and dwells in the second *jhāna*.
mn52:5.3: ‘This second *jhāna* too is constructed and intended …
mn52:5.4: … attains the unsurpassed security from bondage.
mn52:6.1: Furthermore, householder, a *bhikkhu*, with the fading away of *pīti* … enters and dwells in the third *jhāna*.
mn52:6.3: ‘This third *jhāna* too is constructed and intended …
mn52:6.4: … attains the unsurpassed security from bondage.
mn52:7.1: Furthermore, householder, a *bhikkhu*, with the abandoning of *sukha* … enters and dwells in the fourth *jhāna*.
mn52:7.3: ‘This fourth *jhāna* too is constructed and intended …
mn52:7.4: … attains the unsurpassed security from bondage.
mn52:8.1: Furthermore, householder, a *bhikkhu* dwells pervading one direction with a mind imbued with goodwill [*mettā*], likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth. Thus above, below, across, everywhere, and in every respect, he dwells pervading the entire world with a mind imbued with goodwill [*mettā*], abundant, expansive, measureless, free from hostility, free from ill will.
mn52:8.3: ‘This liberation of mind through goodwill [*mettā*] [*mettācetovimutti*] too is constructed and intended.
mn52:8.4: But whatever is constructed and intended, that is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], subject to cessation [*nirodhadhamman*].’ He understands it thus.
mn52:8.6: … attains the unsurpassed security from bondage.
mn52:9.1: Furthermore, householder, a *bhikkhu* dwells pervading with a mind imbued with compassion [*karuṇā*] …
